{{Quote|He's ''Marko Tyne''. ''Wanted'' in nine systems for unlicensed slaving. I'm ''claiming'' him.|Beilert Valance, after killing Tyne|<ref name="SW27" />}}
'''Marko Tyne''' was a [[Zygerrian/Legends|Zygerrian]] active during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], who conducted unlicensed [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] in nine of [[Thesme sector/Legends|Thesme sector's]] [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] at some point<ref name="SW27" /> prior to [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].<ref name="Date" /> For these acts, he was wanted by [[Thesme|Thesmian]] authorities.<ref name="The Hunt Within" /> Not long after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Tyne visited the [[Junction City cantina]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Feriae Junction/Legends|Feriae Junction]]. He was tracked there by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]], who [[Death/Legends|killed]] him with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] shot to the chest, much to the shock of the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] visitors. Claiming the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Tyne's head, Valance forced two cantina patrons to carry the slaver's body out of the establishment.<ref name="SW27" />

A [[Color/Legends|brown]]-[[Hair/Legends|haired]]<ref name="SW27" /> Zygerrian,<ref name="The Hunt Within" /> Tyne engaged in slavery,<ref name="SW27" /> the traditional business of his [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="GaW">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref><ref name="TCW">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Slaves of the Republic]]''</ref> He owned a blaster.<ref name="SW27" />

==Behind the scenes==
Marko Tyne first appeared in the [[Star Wars (1977) 27|twenty-seventh]] issue of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series. The issue was written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]],<ref name="SW27" /> and released on [[June 26]], [[1979]] by [[Marvel Comics]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53817/star_wars_1977_27|text=Star Wars (1977) #27}} {{C|First printing}}</ref> Despite being established as such, Tyne bears no resemblance to the later official [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] appearance of the Zygerrians, lacking any bony spurs on his face.<ref name="GaW" /><ref name="TCW" />
